_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in determining system authorization status
reference_document_element_description: Execute data subject rights requests within regulatory timelines, including identity verification, request scope determination, response preparation, exemption assessment, and fulfillment documentation.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Task RC-T-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-001 (RC, cosine: 0.450), SP-K-005 (SP, cosine: 0.441).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.352). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [authorization, determining, skill, status, system] vs WIDAI-only terms [assessment, data, determination, documentation, execute, exemption] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.878, R=0.846). P exceeds R by 0.032 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (57.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in determining system authorization status" and "Execute data subject rights requests within regulatory timelines, including iden". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.463 > STS 0.352 (gap +0.111). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [authorization, determining, skill, status, system]. WIDAI terms: [assessment, data, determination, documentation, execute, exemption].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 57.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
